questions.statement: What is the maximum attachment size limit for Gmail emails?
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is 25 MB.

Key Points

  • Gmail allows users to attach files up to a maximum size of 25 MB per email.
  • If the attachment exceeds this limit, Gmail provides the option to upload the file to Google Drive and share the link instead.
  • This size limit applies to both the email body and attachments combined, ensuring emails are manageable in size for recipients.
  • For recipients using non-Gmail services, large emails may still be rejected if their service provider has stricter limits.
  • Compressing files into .zip or .rar formats can help reduce file size and meet the 25 MB limit.

Additional Information

  • Alternatives for Sending Large Files:
    • Google Drive: Files larger than 25 MB can be uploaded to Google Drive, and a link can be shared via email.
    • Third-party Services: Platforms like WeTransfer or Dropbox can be used to share larger files.
    • File Compression: Compressing files into smaller formats can help in meeting attachment size requirements.
  • Tips for Efficient Email Management:
    • Regularly clean up your Gmail inbox to free up space and avoid running out of storage.
    • Organize emails using labels and filters for better management of attachments and messages.
    • Use cloud storage solutions for archiving large files instead of keeping them in your inbox.

questions.statement: Select the _____ to see all the slides in your presentation and to quickly jump to another slide.&nbsp;
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is thumbnail icon.

Key Points

  • The thumbnail icon is used to display all the slides in a presentation in a compact visual format.
  • It allows users to quickly navigate between different slides without scrolling through the entire presentation.
  • This feature is especially useful during presentations, enabling seamless transitions to specific slides as needed.
  • The thumbnail view provides an overview of the slide order and content, assisting in better organization and navigation.
  • In most presentation software, the thumbnail icon is located in the toolbar or sidebar, making it easily accessible.

Additional Information

  • Other Common Icons in Presentation Software:
    • Annotations Pen Icon: Used to draw or write on slides during a presentation to emphasize specific points.
    • Magnifying Glass Icon: Allows users to zoom in or out on specific parts of a slide for better visibility.
    • Screen Icon: Typically used to switch between presentation modes, such as full-screen or split-screen view.
  • Best Practices for Presentations:
    • Organize slides in a logical sequence to ensure smooth flow of information.
    • Use the thumbnail view to review the overall structure of the presentation before delivering it.
    • Practice navigating between slides using the thumbnail icon to avoid delays during the presentation.
  • Tips for Effective Slide Design:
    • Keep slide content concise and visually appealing to maintain audience engagement.
    • Use high-quality images and graphics to support the information being presented.
    • Choose fonts and color schemes that are easy to read and professional in appearance.

questions.statement: Which of the following features in MS Word allows you to quickly replace a specific word or phrase throughout a document?
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is Find and Replace.

Key Points

  • Find and Replace is a feature in MS Word that allows users to locate specific words or phrases in a document and replace them with another word or phrase quickly.
  • This tool is especially useful for editing large documents where manual changes would be time-consuming.
  • The Find option searches for a specific string of text, while the Replace option substitutes it with new content.
  • It supports advanced search options like matching case, whole words only, and using wildcards for more precise operations.
  • The Find and Replace feature significantly enhances productivity by streamlining repetitive text editing tasks.

Additional Information

  • Steps to Use Find and Replace:
    • Open the document in MS Word.
    • Press Ctrl + H to open the Find and Replace dialog box.
    • Enter the word or phrase to find in the Find what field.
    • Enter the replacement text in the Replace with field.
    • Click Find Next to locate the text, and then click Replace to substitute it. Use Replace All to update all occurrences at once.
  • Other Features in MS Word:
    • Track Changes: Used to monitor edits in a document, showing who made changes and when.
    • AutoCorrect: Automatically corrects common spelling and formatting errors as you type.
    • Spell Check: Identifies and suggests corrections for spelling and grammatical errors in the document.
  • Advantages of Using Find and Replace:
    • Saves time by automating repetitive text editing tasks.
    • Reduces the risk of missing instances of text that need to be updated.
    • Provides advanced search options for greater flexibility and precision.
  • Important Tips:
    • Always review changes made using Replace All to ensure accuracy.
    • Use the Match case and Find whole words only options for more specific results.
    • Back up your document before making extensive changes to avoid accidental data loss.

questions.statement: Which of the following applications of computers has significantly impacted the field of personalised medicine?&nbsp;
options: Analysis of individual patient genomic data to tailor treatments /
Geographic Information Systems (GIS) for mapping environmental data /
Automated Teller Machines (ATMs) for financial transactions /
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is Analysis of individual patient genomic data to tailor treatments.

Key Points

  • Personalised medicine leverages computer technologies to analyze individual genomic data, enabling tailored treatments for patients.
  • This approach helps in identifying specific genetic mutations or markers that may influence the effectiveness of drugs or therapies.
  • Advanced computational tools are used to process vast amounts of genomic data, providing insights into patient-specific treatment plans.
  • It has led to significant breakthroughs in treating diseases such as cancer, rare genetic disorders, and chronic illnesses.
  • By integrating computer applications, personalised medicine enhances the precision, efficiency, and outcomes of healthcare treatments.

Additional Information

  • Applications of Personalised Medicine:
    • Pharmacogenomics: Tailoring drug treatments based on individual genetic variations to enhance efficacy and reduce side effects.
    • Genetic Testing: Identifying predispositions to specific diseases, enabling early interventions and preventive measures.
    • Targeted Therapies: Developing treatments that specifically target the genetic mutations causing a disease.
    • Companion Diagnostics: Using diagnostic tests to determine the suitability of a particular treatment for a patient.
  • Role of Computers in Personalised Medicine:
    • High-speed data analysis for processing large genomic datasets efficiently.
    • Integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) to identify patterns and predict treatment outcomes.
    • Development of bioinformatics tools for genome sequencing and annotation.
    • Facilitating real-time monitoring of patients through wearable devices and health apps.
  • Advantages of Personalised Medicine:
    • Improved treatment accuracy and reduced trial-and-error prescribing.
    • Minimization of adverse drug reactions and side effects.
    • Better patient outcomes and quality of life through customized care.
    • Cost-effectiveness by avoiding ineffective treatments and hospitalizations.
  • Challenges in Personalised Medicine:
    • High costs associated with genomic sequencing and data analysis.
    • Ethical and privacy concerns related to the use of genetic data.
    • Need for specialized training and resources in healthcare systems.
    • Limited accessibility in low-resource settings due to infrastructure constraints.

questions.statement: In modern CPUs, what is the role of cache memory?
options: To serve as an alternative to RAM /
To store frequently accessed instructions for faster processing /
To process and execute instructions directly /
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is To store frequently accessed instructions for faster processing.

Key Points

  • Cache memory is a small-sized type of volatile computer memory that provides high-speed data access to the processor.
  • Its primary role is to store frequently accessed instructions and data to reduce the time required for CPU processing.
  • By holding these frequently used instructions closer to the processor, cache memory significantly improves the overall system performance and speed.
  • There are different levels of cache memory, such as L1, L2, and L3, which vary in size and proximity to the CPU. L1 is the fastest and closest to the processor.
  • Cache memory operates faster than RAM and is critical for modern CPUs to handle complex tasks efficiently.

Additional Information

  • Types of Cache Memory:
    • Instruction Cache: Stores instructions that the processor is likely to execute next, speeding up the execution process.
    • Data Cache: Holds data that the CPU frequently accesses during program execution.
    • Unified Cache: Combines both instruction and data caches into a single cache, optimizing usage.
  • Benefits of Cache Memory:
    • Reduces latency by minimizing the time required to access data.
    • Improves CPU performance and efficiency by avoiding frequent access to slower main memory.
    • Enables faster execution of applications and multitasking processes.
  • Important Points:
    • Cache memory is limited in size compared to RAM but is much faster.
    • Effective cache management is crucial to ensure optimal CPU performance.
    • Modern processors use advanced algorithms to manage cache memory dynamically.
